steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: Left side: “the average summer temperature” — a noun phrase serving as the subject. Right side: “just 66.7 degrees Fahrenheit” — a noun phrase serving as the complement, not an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A verb connects directly to its complement without punctuation.
A semicolon separates independent clauses, a colon introduces elaboration or a list, and a comma between verb and complement violates conventions—all incorrect here. That matches choice A — A is correct.
